memoryArtifacts.headline: Family of Herbert from Annals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777
memoryArtifacts.description: Family of Herbert from Annuals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777. However, it is noted, that Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) was not the Count of Vermandois, but rather his uncle was, Herbert IV, Count of Vermandois. It is also noted that the son of Herbertus Camerarius, Henry FitzHerbert (Henry the Treasurer) is missing from the Annals and Antiquities, but his grandson Herbert (Herbert FitzHenry) and great grandson Herbert  (Herbert FitzHerbert) are included.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Pedigree of the family of Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) in Collections Historical & Archaeological Relating to Montgomeryshire, Vol. V, pages 158-159

memoryArtifacts.headline: Herbert the Chamberlain (Herbertus Camerarius), in A Genealogical and Heraldic History of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain and Ireland, Vol. 1, pg. 613 [but missing the generation of Henry the Treasurer)
memoryArtifacts.description: Herbert the Chamberlain (Herbertus Camerarius), in A Genealogical and Heraldic History of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain and Ireland, Vol. 1, pg. 613 [but missing the generation of Henry the Treasurer). The record skips to the son of Henry, Herbert FitzHenry (Herbert FitzHerbert I, and his son Herbert FitzHerbert (II).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Gwilym ap Jenkin於1327年出生在威爾士蒙姆爾時，他的父親Jenken Ap Adam26歲，母親Gwenlian Verch Aron22歲。他與Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他在威爾士蒙姆爾阿加文尼被列為employment。他去世於大約1377年在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾享年51歲，埋葬在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾。

nameMeanings.name: Jenkin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal names Jankin, Jenkin, and Jonkin, pet forms of Jan, Jen, and Jon + the hypocoristic suffix -kin. In Middle English, names in -kin were often used to name the younger of two family members with the same personal name.

variants.name: Jenkins
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Jenkin , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. Jenkins is one of the most common surnames in England, especially southwestern England, but is also especially associated with Wales.

variants.name: Jenks
meanings: English (West Midlands): from the Middle English personal name Jenke (a shortening of the personal name Jenkin, Jankin; see Jenkin ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

History: Joseph Jenks (1602–83), the descendant of an old Welsh family, was born in England and traveled to Saugus, near Lynn, MA, in 1642 to assist in the development of America's first iron works. His son, Joseph Jenckes (sic), followed in 1650, founded Pawtucket, RI, and raised four sons who held places of respect and distinction in RI, including one who served as governor for five years.

variants.name: Jenkinson
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): from the Middle English personal names Jankin, Jenkin, or Jonkin (see Jenkin ) + son.

nameMeanings.name: Gwilym
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of William , in use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Gwilim
meanings: equivalent of William , in use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Gwillym
meanings: equivalent of William , in use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Gwilym ap Jenkin was born in 1327, in Monmouthshire, Wales, his father, Jenken Ap Adam, was 26 and his mother, Gwenlian Verch Aron, was 22. He married Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 5 daughters. His occupation is listed as employment in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales. He died about 1377, in Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 51, and was buried in Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gwilym ap Jenkin est né en 1327, à Monmouthshire, Galles, son père, Jenken Ap Adam, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Gwenlian Verch Aron, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est employment à Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles. Il est décédé environ 1377, à Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterré à Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gwilym ap Jenkin 1327 in Monmouthshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jenken Ap Adam 26 und seine Mutter Gwenlian Verch Aron 22. Er hat Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 5 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als employment in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales aufgeführt. Er starb um 1377 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom und wurde in Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 5 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come employment nel Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles. Morì circa 1377, nel Monmouthshire, Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolto a Monmouthshire, Galles, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Gwilym ap Jenkinが1327年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアで生まれた時，父Jenken Ap Adamは26歳，母Gwenlian Verch Aronは22歳であった。Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。ウェールズモンモウスシュアイアアバーガベニーでの職業はemploymentと記録されている。彼は約1377年にイギリスウェールズモンモウスシュアイアで51歳で死去し，イギリスウェールズモンモウスシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gwilym ap Jenkin(이)가 1327년 웨일스 몬모우스실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jenken Ap Adam(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Gwenlian Verch Aron(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 웨일스 몬모우스실르 압르가븐이에서 employment이었다. 그는 대략 1377년 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gwilym ap Jenkin родился в 1327 г. в местности монмоутшир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Jenken Ap Adam было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Gwenlian Verch Aron, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей.  Он работал по профессии employment в местности абиргавенни, монмоутшир, Уэльс. Он умер около 1377 г. в местности монмоутшир, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 51 год и был похоронен в местности монмоутшир, Уэльс, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gwilym ap Jenkin nació en 1327, en Monmouthshire, Gales, su papá, Jenken Ap Adam, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Gwenlian Verch Aron, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 5 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era employment en Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales. Murió aproximadamente 1377, en Monmouthshire, Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultado en Monmouthshire, Gales, Reino Unido.
